name: Altair (Vega-Lite Python)

performance.notes: Data must fit in browser/notebook memory; large datasets require sampling

agent_readiness.notes: Python library for declarative statistical visualization based on Vega-Lite. Grammar of graphics approach. Concise, readable chart specifications. Excellent composability (facets, layers, concatenation). Interactive selections and filtering. Jupyter notebook integration. Outputs JSON spec renderable in any Vega-Lite-capable viewer. Best Python choice for exploratory visualization with clean API.

gotchas: 5,000 row default limit — must set altair.data_transformers.enable('vegafusion') for large data
JSON schema embedded in output — not suitable for very large datasets
Less performant than Plotly for dynamic/interactive web apps
best_when: Exploratory data analysis in Jupyter notebooks
Concise, declarative statistical charts (histograms, scatter, heatmaps)
Academic data visualization with publication-quality output
avoid_when: Very large datasets without VegaFusion backend
Production web app dashboards (use Plotly/D3.js directly)
summary: Altair is Python's declarative statistical visualization library based on Vega-Lite. Grammar of graphics approach with clean, concise code. Excellent composability (facets, layers, concatenation). Interactive selections. Jupyter-native. Best Python choice for exploratory analysis and agent-generated charts. 5,000 row default limit — enable VegaFusion for large datasets.
